hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 contacts, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding unfavorable interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but lack sufficient selectivity. The next iteration should focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while maintaining the core pharmacophore. This will help increase the Δmargin and achieve the primary objective of SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing design efforts here can help disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).

Introducing charged or small hydrophobic residues at these positions can disrupt conserved interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining overall peptide stability and pharmacophore integrity.
